title: Japanese Nabemono with Celery and Seared Beef
description: A traditional Japanese hot pot dish featuring seared beef, celery, and a variety of vegetables served with a savory broth.
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp of oil in the pan over medium heat.
Add the ginger and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the soy sauce, sake, and sugar, and stir to combine.
Add 4 cups of nabemono broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the beef and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until seared to desired level of doneness.
Add the celery and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until tender.
Add the mushrooms and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until tender.
Serve the hot pot with scallions and additional nabemono broth, if desired.
Cook the beef to an internal temperature of 130°F for medium-rare, 140°F for medium, and 150°F for medium-well.
Let the beef rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
